kind.doc: Player aim control. In normal third-person play the aim is camera-derived.

associated_functions.name: GetAdjustedCameraMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Returns the matrix used by the aim raycast: in aim-down-sights it reads the alternate-aim
 transform, otherwise the camera transform. `weapon` adds an optional ballistic pitch. The
 result is written through `result` and also returned.

associated_functions.name: UpdateDirectAim
associated_functions.doc: Raycasts from the camera position along camera-forward to find the aim target.

associated_functions.name: GetGripPosition
associated_functions.doc: Writes the grip transform for the given `hand` (a `E_WEAPON_GRIP_HAND_*` value).

kind.doc: An Autodesk HumanIK wrapper.

associated_functions.name: AddEffectorTargetPosition
associated_functions.doc:
Pushes an IK effector target, in character-local space. `solve_step` and `pass_info` are the
 `HIKSolveStep` and `HIKPassInfo` enums.

 **Provenance:** the prototype is verified against the debug PDB.

kind.doc:
The animation-system event-id (act) symbol table: maps act names (`ACT_*`) to the sequential
 runtime ids that the animation state machines and [`Character::QueueAct`](character::character::Character::QueueAct)
 use. Ids are assigned in registration order as names are first encountered (by loaded animation
 data or the `NCharacter` id globals), so an act's id is only resolvable at runtime.

associated_functions.name: string_to_id
associated_functions.doc:
Resolves an act name to its runtime id. Registers the name with a fresh id when it is not
 yet present (asserting if the table has been locked), so resolving a name that no loaded
 animation data uses yields an id that no queued act will ever carry.

kind.doc:
The on-foot / land-vehicle reversing act: drives the `S_REVERSE_*` states in
 `rico_base.afsmb`, where Rico turns to look over his shoulder while backing up.
The motorbike variant of [`ACT_REVERSE`](EventIdSymbolTable::ACT_REVERSE).

A keyed value store attached to game objects; the character's instance lives at
 `Character + 0x2060` and carries the locomotion values (the camera-relative move direction, the
 target face direction, the up direction). Values are read and written by id through the
 template accessors below.

associated_functions.name: GetVector3
associated_functions.doc:
The `GetBlackboardValue<CVector3f>` template instantiation. Returns whether the id was
 present; the value is written through `value`.

associated_functions.name: GetFloat
associated_functions.doc: The `GetBlackboardValue<float>` template instantiation.

associated_functions.name: SetVector3
associated_functions.doc:
The `SetBlackboardValue<CVector3f>` template instantiation. `value` is by-value `CVector3f`
 at the C++ level, which the MSVC x64 ABI passes by reference — hence the pointer here.
 `flags` is `1` at every observed call site; `name` is an optional debug label, null in
 release calls.

kind.doc:
The id of the second float the input move task reads alongside
 [`SPEED_ID`](ObjectBlackboard::SPEED_ID); semantics unmapped, a candidate input-strength
 signal.

The id of the constrained-ground movement direction (`CVector3f`), present on slopes (and
 other surface-constrained locomotion): when set, the movement task blends its displacement
 direction toward it instead of using the primary displacement output.

The id of the camera-relative world-space move direction (`CVector3f`), written each frame
 by `NStateTask_InputLocoSetTargetDirTask::SetupTargetDir`.

The id of the float speed value the input locomotion tasks branch on (`<= 0` routes into
 the stop acts).

The id of the target face direction (`CVector3f`): the desired body facing the orientation
 executor yaws toward in its tracking mode. Written per-state by the game's
 `SetUpTargetFaceDir` tasks.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame camera update: snapshots the previous view-projection, lerps the transform from
 `m_TransformT0` to `m_TransformT1` by `dtf`, and recalculates the projection and frustum.

associated_functions.name: SetupRenderCamera
associated_functions.doc:
Builds the render-camera matrices once per draw: applies reverse-Z and the TAA jitter to the
 projection, then rebuilds `m_ViewProjection` and `m_ViewProjectionF` from `m_View`. Runs at most
 once per camera, guarded by the `m_IsRenderCamera` flag. Returns the last matrix-copy
 destination.

associated_functions.name: SetComputeView
associated_functions.doc:
Sets or clears the `m_ComputeView` flag. When set, [`UpdateRender`](Camera::UpdateRender)
 re-derives `m_View` as the inverse of `m_TransformF` each frame instead of reading a supplied
 view matrix.

associated_functions.name: RecalcProjection
associated_functions.doc:
Rebuilds `m_Projection` from the FOV, near, far, and aspect parameters (perspective,
 off-center, or orthographic per the `m_UseOffCenter` and `m_Ortho` flags), clears
 `m_DirtyProjection`, and applies the reverse-Z remap only when `m_IsRenderCamera` is set.

kind.doc: The opaque per-frame camera pipeline context.

fields.name: m_ActiveCamera
fields.doc:
The camera gameplay drives. Sim-side systems read it -- the sun-shadow fit
 ([`ShadowManager::UpdateRender`](graphics_engine::shadow_manager::ShadowManager::UpdateRender))
 among them.

associated_functions.name: InitTransform
associated_functions.doc:
Sets the active camera's transform, writing both `m_TransformT0` and `m_TransformT1` from `mat`
 so the subsequent interpolation holds it constant.

associated_functions.name: InitFOV
associated_functions.doc: Sets the active camera's field of view, both the T0 and T1 values.

associated_functions.name: GetRenderCamera
associated_functions.doc: Returns the camera the render thread reads.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
The sim-path per-frame update: iterates every camera in the manager's list and calls
 [`Camera::UpdateRender`] on each. This is where the active camera's `m_View` is produced for the
 frame.

kind.doc:
The root of the camera pipeline tree. Flattens and weights the pipelines and runs their render
 modifiers, populating the render contexts each frame.
An opaque node in the camera pipeline.

associated_functions.name: PushRenderContext
associated_functions.doc:
Funnels the camera control contexts into the active camera state: reads the next render
 context, sets the audio listener, applies the jitter filter, and calls
 [`CameraManager::InitTransform`] and [`CameraManager::InitFOV`].

associated_functions.name: UpdateBlackboardValues
associated_functions.doc: Reads the action-effector inputs into the transformed-gamepad-input blackboard value.

associated_functions.name: IsInCinematicCamera
associated_functions.doc: Whether the currently selected camera in the director stack has the cinematic flag set.

associated_functions.name: IsAlternateAimTransformUsed
associated_functions.doc: Whether the alternate-aim (aim-down-sights) transform is in use.

associated_functions.name: GetInputMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Writes the camera matrix used for mapping player input to world space — the same matrix the
 locomotion input task reads to make the move direction camera-relative (its negated third
 row is the camera forward on the ground plane). Reads
 [`CameraControlContext::m_NextRenderContext`](camera::camera_context::CameraControlContext::m_NextRenderContext)'s camera transform.

associated_functions.name: GetCameraMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Writes the sim-phase camera matrix: [`CameraControlContext::m_NextCameraContext`](camera::camera_context::CameraControlContext::m_NextCameraContext)'s camera
 transform, as opposed to [`GetInputMatrix`](GameCameraManager::GetInputMatrix)'s next *render* context. Read by the player aim
 control (raycast start position, adjusted camera matrix, target visibility casts), weapon
 aim-target queries, melee and grapple state tasks, and other sim-side camera consumers.

associated_functions.name: GetAlternateAimMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Writes the alternate-aim (aim-down-sights) matrix:
 [`CameraControlContext::m_NextRenderContext`](camera::camera_context::CameraControlContext::m_NextRenderContext)'s alternate aim transform. The player aim
 control prefers it over [`GetCameraMatrix`](GameCameraManager::GetCameraMatrix) when [`IsAlternateAimTransformUsed`](GameCameraManager::IsAlternateAimTransformUsed) is set.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame render update: runs the camera tree via
 [`CameraTree::UpdateRenderContexts`], pushes the render context, then updates the lighting and
 water level at the camera.

kind.doc: The camera arm, rotated by the look delta.

associated_functions.name: DeltaTransform
associated_functions.doc: Applies a `(yaw, pitch, roll)` delta-angle to the boom.

kind.doc:
Converts the look effectors into a camera orbit delta-angle and applies it to the [`BoomTransform`]
 camera arm.

associated_functions.name: CalculateInputDeltaAngles
associated_functions.doc:
Reads the look effectors, combines them, applies per-axis sensitivity, and returns the
 `(yaw, pitch)` delta-angle.

associated_functions.name: ProcessCameraContext
associated_functions.doc:
The caller of [`CalculateInputDeltaAngles`](InputToOrbitModifier::CalculateInputDeltaAngles):
 takes the returned delta-angle and applies it to the camera via
 [`BoomTransform::DeltaTransform`].

kind.doc:
The character's aiming state, written each frame by
 [`HandleAimingInputPlayer`](Character::HandleAimingInputPlayer) for the player. The locomotion
 state machine reads it to choose aim-relative (strafe) movement over run/steer:
 `m_AimingWeapon` (or `m_AimingGrapple`) is the switch that makes the body face the aim direction
 and turns the directional keys into strafes. `m_WasAiming` reflects the state as of the start of
 this frame. Bits `0x01`/`0x02` and `0x40`/`0x80` are unmapped.

fields.name: m_ModelInstances
fields.doc:
The model-instance slots (`NModelSystem::CModelInstance*`, null when unused): the base
 body model plus attachments and variants. `CAnimatedModel::GetModel`/`SetModel` index this
 array directly. Each instance embeds its render-block-instance info (`CRBIInfo`) at
 instance + 0x50, which is the `info` pointer its render blocks are drawn with.

associated_functions.name: TryAct
associated_functions.doc:
Tests whether the animation state machine would accept the act from its current state,
 without queuing it. `CCharacter`'s vtable slot 37 forwards to this on
 [`Character::m_AnimatedModel`]; the game's own act dispatchers call it before
 [`Character::QueueAct`] to pick a supported act.

kind.doc:
The [`m_ModelInstances`](AnimatedModel::m_ModelInstances) slot holding the character's
 *body* model -- the one whose skeleton the character's bone indices belong to. The other
 slots are attachments with their own skeletons (the parachute among them), where the same
 numeric indices land on arbitrary bones.

The offset of a model instance's embedded render-block-instance info (`CRBIInfo`) within
 `NModelSystem::CModelInstance` (`CModelInstance::AddToPass` passes `this + 0x50` to
 `ForEachRb`); that pointer is the `info` every one of its block draws receives. A constant
 rather than a field because the instance type itself is unmapped.

The number of [`m_ModelInstances`](AnimatedModel::m_ModelInstances) slots. Keep in sync
 with that array's length.

fields.name: m_Blackboard
fields.doc:
The character's embedded [`ObjectBlackboard`] (`lea rcx, [character+2060h]` at every
 blackboard call site in the loco tasks). The blackboard type is opaque, so this zero-size
 field only anchors the offset: take its address to obtain the blackboard pointer.

fields.name: m_AimFlags
fields.doc: Packed aiming-state bit-flags; see [`AimState`].

fields.name: m_AimTimer
fields.doc:
The weapon-aim countdown timer, in seconds. It is refreshed while an aim input is held and
 decremented by the frame delta otherwise; a positive value keeps
 [`m_AimingWeapon`](AimState::m_AimingWeapon) set in [`m_AimFlags`](Character::m_AimFlags),
 so the character stays in aim/strafe locomotion for a short tail after the aim button is
 released.

associated_functions.name: UpdatePropEffects
associated_functions.doc: The per-frame update of the character's attached prop visual effects.

associated_functions.name: HandleAimingInputPlayer
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame player aiming-input update. Resolves whether the player is aiming a weapon or
 grapple from the equipped weapon and [`m_AimTimer`](Character::m_AimTimer), then writes the
 result into [`m_AimFlags`](Character::m_AimFlags)
 ([`m_AimingWeapon`](AimState::m_AimingWeapon) / [`m_AimingGrapple`](AimState::m_AimingGrapple)).
 The locomotion tasks read those flags to select aim-relative (strafe) movement. NPCs use a
 separate `HandleAimingInputNPC`; this variant is player-only and is the single point that
 decides the on-foot aim/strafe state.

associated_functions.name: IsAimingWeaponPossible
associated_functions.doc:
Whether the character is currently permitted to aim a weapon (an aimable weapon is equipped
 and the character state allows it). Gates the aim path in the locomotion tasks.

associated_functions.name: IsPlayer
associated_functions.doc:
Whether the character is the local player. Used by the locomotion queue helpers to restrict
 the aim-relative movement path to the player.

associated_functions.name: QueueAct
associated_functions.doc:
Queues an animation act by id (`act` points at the runtime `idstring` value, e.g. the
 `ACT_*` id globals initialized at startup). Acts drive the animation state machine's
 transitions; unmatched acts are dropped at the end of the frame.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame tick. Measures the `QueryPerformanceCounter` delta and updates `m_SPF` (an
 exponential moving average), `m_RealSPF`, `m_FPS`, and `m_GameTimeMicro`.

fields.name: m_Weather
fields.doc: The live weather, also returned by [`GetWeather`](Atmosphere::GetWeather).

associated_functions.name: Apply
associated_functions.doc:
The in-engine reference that reads and writes both the time of day and the weather each cycle:
 the canonical example of driving both systems.

kind.doc:
The live weather state: a continuous scalar model.

 **Caution:** [`WeatherController`]'s per-frame update overwrites these scalars toward its own
 targets unless a force flag or a named weather event holds them, so prefer firing an event (see
 [`WeatherController::Init`]) to pin a state.

fields.name: m_SnowRatio
fields.doc:
The snow blend, in `0..1`.

 **Unverified:** from the dump's struct layout, not instruction-verified.

fields.name: m_RainIntensity
fields.doc:
The rain intensity, in `0..1`.

 **Unverified:** from the dump's struct layout, not instruction-verified.

fields.name: m_Severity
fields.doc: The storm severity, from roughly `0.1` (clear) to `4.0` (full storm).

associated_functions.name: SetSeverity
associated_functions.doc:
Sets `m_Severity` and clears the severity-update flag. See the type's caution about the
 controller overwriting it.

associated_functions.name: UpdateSeverityTarget
associated_functions.doc: Smoothly drives the severity toward `target` over `update_time` seconds.

associated_functions.name: Init
associated_functions.doc:
Subscribes the named weather events (the `EVENT_*` constants, plus `cloud_base` and
 `cloud_height`). Firing one via the engine's event send is the robust way to hold a weather
 state, since the controller's per-frame update otherwise overwrites the [`Weather`]
 scalars.

kind.doc: Applies the pinned state instantly rather than blending toward it.
Pins rain (severity `4.0`, rain intensity `1.0`, snow `0`).
Hands control back to the ambient weather system.
Pins snow (severity `4.0`, snow ratio `1.0`).
Pins clear weather (severity `0.1`).

fields.name: m_CurrentTimeOfDay
fields.doc:
The current time of day, in hours. The render engine copies this out each frame, so a write
 here propagates to lighting.

associated_functions.name: SetTimeOfDay
associated_functions.doc:
Sets the time of day in hours: wraps to a 24-hour range, fires the per-hour event, and clamps
 against `m_PauseTimeOfDay`. Preferred over writing `m_CurrentTimeOfDay` directly.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc: The top-level per-frame update: advances the sim and drives rendering. Returns false on exit.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc: Builds the per-frame update contexts and drives rendering.

kind.doc:
Parameters to [`CreateFragmentProgram`]: the compiled DXBC bytecode (`m_Code`) and its byte length
 (`m_Size`), passed straight through to `ID3D11Device::CreatePixelShader`. `m_Size` is read as a
 pointer-width value (the bytecode length argument to `CreatePixelShader`).

The primitive topology passed to the draw wrappers. The patchlist variants are tessellation
 control-point counts.
The global-illumination render pass (`RP_GLOBAL_ILLUMINATION`), owning the LPV [`GISolver`].

fields.name: m_pGISolver
fields.doc: The LPV solver the pass delegates its GI work to.

kind.doc:
The light-propagation-volume solver: RSM inject + LPV propagation for the global-illumination pass.
 `CGISolver::Execute` refreshes one of the two LPV cascades each dispatch and toggles
 [`m_CascadeToUpdate`](GISolver::m_CascadeToUpdate) afterward, so the cascades are refreshed in
 alternation across frames.

fields.name: m_CascadeToUpdate
fields.doc:
Selects which of the two LPV cascades is freshly injected + propagated this dispatch. Toggles
 `0 <-> 1` once per `Execute` (i.e. once per `Draw`).

kind.doc:
The scene light manager, owning the global-illumination pass. The singleton address holds a pointer
 to the manager, which is null until the light system initializes.

fields.name: m_GIPass
fields.doc: The global-illumination render pass.

kind.doc:
The opaque parameter block passed to
 [`HandleDrawThreadTask`](GraphicsEngine::HandleDrawThreadTask).

One reflection-proxy depth-history slot in the planar / water reflection state machine. Five of
 these live on the graphics engine; the lifecycle byte advances once per scene dispatch.

fields.name: m_DepthTexture
fields.doc: The reflection-proxy depth texture.

fields.name: m_FrameIndex
fields.doc:
The lifecycle counter: `0` is free, `2` promotes to `3`, `3` is picked, otherwise it
 increments.

fields.name: m_DrawThreadWorkSignal
fields.doc:
Completion signal for the async draw-dispatch CPU fragment that
 [`DispatchDraw`](GraphicsEngine::DispatchDraw) kicks to run the render passes. The fragment sets
 it non-zero on completion; the engine waits on it (via
 `cpu_fragment::CpuFragmentWaitUntilSignalIsNonZero`, gated by `CpuPrimaryCount() > 1`) only at the
 *next* [`Draw`](GraphicsEngine::Draw)'s entry.
 [`WaitForCPUDrawToFinish`](GraphicsEngine::WaitForCPUDrawToFinish) does *not* wait on it.

fields.name: m_ShadowManager
fields.doc: The cascaded sun-shadow system.

fields.name: m_RenderCamera
fields.doc:
The engine-owned scene render camera: a by-value copy rebuilt each
 [`Draw`](GraphicsEngine::Draw) by `Camera::SetupRenderCamera` (reverse-Z + jitter, then the
 view-projection products from `m_View`). This is the camera the render passes consume;
 distinct from the `CameraManager`'s camera objects, which are pointers to the gameplay
 cameras.

fields.name: m_GBufferTexture
fields.doc: Deferred-shading render targets ("GBuffer0".."GBuffer3").

fields.name: m_VelocityBufferTexture
fields.doc: Motion-blur velocity buffer.

fields.name: m_MainDepthTexture
fields.doc: Main scene depth ("MainDepthBuffer").

fields.name: m_MainColorBuffer
fields.doc: Main scene HDR color ("MainColorBuffer").

fields.name: m_DownSampledDepthTexture
fields.doc: Hi-Z / downsampled depth ("DownsampledDepth").

fields.name: m_EffectInfo
fields.doc: Reflection-proxy depth-history (planar / water reflection state machine), 5 slots.

fields.name: m_BackBufferLinear
fields.doc: Final linear back buffer ("BackBufferLinear").

fields.name: m_DrawScene
fields.doc:
Whether [`HandleDrawThreadTask`](GraphicsEngine::HandleDrawThreadTask) renders the 3D scene
 this frame (GBuffer, world, post-effects) rather than only the UI. [`Draw`](GraphicsEngine::Draw)
 sets it from the game state and the UI's static-background grab: it is cleared while a
 full-screen UI with a static background is shown (pause / map / menus), in which case the draw
 thread clears the target to transparent instead of rendering the scene.

fields.name: m_EffectInfoIndex
fields.doc: Index of the reflection-proxy slot picked this frame.

fields.name: m_CurrentBundleName
fields.doc:
The currently-loaded shader bundle name, compared by
 [`LoadShaderBundle`](GraphicsEngine::LoadShaderBundle) to skip a same-name reload. The
 bundle names are `"Shaders"` / `"ShadersLowShadows"` (and the Intel `"ShadersConstMath*"`
 variants).

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc:
The graphics entry point: runs the per-frame prologue (presents the previous frame, advances
 the clock and constant-buffer pools), then dispatches this frame's draw.

associated_functions.name: Flip
associated_functions.doc: Presents the previous frame's back buffer.

associated_functions.name: DispatchDraw
associated_functions.doc: Queues this frame's render work on the render thread.

associated_functions.name: HandleDrawThreadTask
associated_functions.doc: The render-thread body: GBuffer, lighting, post-effects, and UI.

associated_functions.name: TextureCachePlatformUpdate
associated_functions.doc:
A draw-prologue step. Copies the active camera into the engine-owned render-camera slot, runs
 [`Camera::SetupRenderCamera`] on it, publishes it as the camera manager's render camera, then
 runs the per-frame texture-cache update under the context lock.

associated_functions.name: LoadShaderBundle
associated_functions.doc:
Loads (or reloads) the named shader bundle and re-creates every shader holder from it, but only
 if `name` differs from [`m_CurrentBundleName`](GraphicsEngine::m_CurrentBundleName).
 Re-creating the holders routes every shader through
 `Graphics::CreateFragmentProgram`. The bundle names are `"Shaders"` / `"ShadersLowShadows"`
 (and the Intel `"ShadersConstMath*"` variants), selected by shadow quality in
 `CSettingsManager::UpdateSettings`. `name` is a NUL-terminated C string.

kind.doc: A GPU context handle.
A GPU device handle.
A render-target configuration a pass draws into.
A GPU texture handle.

The per-view render context the render passes read: the camera matrices (view, projection, the
 translation-free offset view-projection, and the separate camera world position), shadow data, and
 per-frame flags. Filled each dispatch by [`RenderPass::SetRenderContextCamera`].

fields.name: m_TransformIndex
fields.doc:
The 8 per-atlas-slice projective shadow transforms, copied per dispatch from the shadow
 manager's parity storage. The deferred lighting shaders index them dynamically by a light's
 packed slice index (`cb0[63 + 4*slice .. 66 + 4*slice]` in the GlobalConstants) to project a
 light-relative position into its shadow-atlas slice; the sun resolve uses
 [`m_ShadowCascades`](RenderContext::m_ShadowCascades) instead.
 The instance-transform slot the render blocks pass to
 [`RBIInfo::GetMatrix`](graphics_engine::render_block::RBIInfo::GetMatrix) for the current
 dispatch.

fields.name: m_ShadowCascades
fields.doc: The forward-material cascaded sun-shadow transform + cascade box-test parameters.

fields.name: m_ActiveCascadeCount
fields.doc:
The number of active cascades this frame, copied per dispatch from the shadow manager's
 parity storage (a byte store in [`RenderPass::SetRenderContextCamera`]).

fields.name: m_RenderStatus
fields.doc:
The pass-family status bits for the current draw: `0x1` default, `0x2` static shadow map,
 `0x4` dynamic shadow map. Render blocks branch on `& 6` to select the shadow/depth-only
 path versus the full material path.

kind.doc:
The cascaded sun-shadow constants for the forward-material resolve: the cascade transform plus the
 per-cascade box-test parameters, staged into cb0 by `RenderEngine::SetGlobalShaderConstants`.

fields.name: m_Transform
fields.doc:
Maps a camera-relative world position into cascade/texture space (row-major, row-vector; its
 columns are `cb0[45..47]` in the GlobalConstants, the translation `cb0[48]`). The
 forward-material shadow resolve evaluates `(worldPos - cameraPos) * M + translation`, with the
 camera position from `cb0[4]`; `CShadowManager::UpdateCascade` bakes the transform relative to
 the active camera's position.

fields.name: m_ScaleBlend
fields.doc: Per-cascade scale + blend-band (`.w`), one per cascade.

fields.name: m_OffsetRadius
fields.doc: Per-cascade offset + texture-array slice (`.w`), one per cascade.

kind.doc: Anti-aliasing resolve mode.

The anti-aliasing resolve. [`AAMode::AA_SMAA_T2X`] additionally reprojects against a previous-frame
 history texture.

associated_functions.name: Apply
associated_functions.doc: `slot` is the in/out post-effect result-slot index.

associated_functions.name: ApplySubsampleJitter
associated_functions.doc:
Post-multiplies the sub-pixel clip-space jitter translation onto `proj`, only when the resolve
 mode is [`AAMode::AA_SMAA_T2X`]. The phase comes from the previous-frame counter parity.

associated_functions.name: CreateRenderTargetResources
associated_functions.doc:
Allocates the temporal history ping-pong textures and their render setups, sized `width` by
 `height`.

kind.doc: The Gaussian blur, used on the non-bokeh path.

The bokeh blur, used when [`PostEffectsManager::IsBokehActive`]; runs after
 [`DownScale2x2PackFocus`].
The bokeh depth-of-field downscale prepass: a 2x2 pack plus focus.
The alpha-blended fade quad over the scene.
The bloom / glare generator. Writes its own scratch targets, composited later.
The red damage vignette. Returns the input slot index unchanged.

fields.name: m_AutoExposureKey
fields.doc:
The auto-exposure target numerator. [`ToneMappingEffect::Update`] sets the exposure target to
 this divided by the raw-brightness histogram mid-point.

kind.doc: Per-frame render-context flags carried by a [`PostEffectRenderContext`].

associated_functions.name: ApplyWorldFilters
associated_functions.doc:
Enqueues the world post-effect block, then steps the world fade accumulator
 ([`ApplyWorldFadeFilter`](PostEffectsManager::ApplyWorldFadeFilter)). `dt` flows only into
 that accumulator; the texture arguments are the scene inputs.

associated_functions.name: ApplyGlobalFilters
associated_functions.doc:
Enqueues the global post-effect block and advances its `dt`-driven accumulators: the screen
 fade alpha and the sun-direction / heat-haze accumulator.

associated_functions.name: ApplyWorldFadeFilter
associated_functions.doc: Steps the world fade accumulator.

associated_functions.name: IsBokehActive
associated_functions.doc:
Whether the bokeh depth-of-field path is active, selecting the downscale plus bokeh blur over
 the plain [`BlurEffect`].

associated_functions.name: IsMotionBlurActive
associated_functions.doc: Whether motion blur is active.

associated_functions.name: ApplySubsampleJitter
associated_functions.doc:
Post-multiplies the temporal sub-pixel jitter onto `proj`. Effective only when the resolve mode
 is [`AAMode::AA_SMAA_T2X`].

kind.doc:
The render block for the post-effects pass. Its [`Draw`](RenderBlockPostEffects::Draw) runs the
 HDR post chain in order: histogram generation, sun-halo pre-apply, blur (bokeh or plain), glare,
 depth of field, motion blur, the HDR-to-LDR tonemap, the player-damage vignette, anti-aliasing,
 sun halo, and the final fade.

 It threads a single result-texture slot index through the slot-returning effects
 ([`DepthOfFieldEffect`], [`MotionBlurEffect`], [`PlayerDamageEffect`], [`AntiAliasingEffect`]),
 hopping between the three fullscreen temp textures.

The sun halo. [`PreApply`](SunHaloEffect::PreApply) prepares it and sets the ready flag;
 [`Apply`](SunHaloEffect::Apply) composites it.

A 3x4 skinning-palette bone matrix. The layout differs per render block (empirically, by the
 block's vertex format/shader variant): some blocks store four 3-float columns with the
 translation in the final three floats, others three 4-float rows with the translation in each
 row's fourth element. The 3x3 rotation is orthonormal under the correct reading, which is how
 a consumer can detect the layout. The skinning palette is an array of these, one per skeleton
 bone, built per frame by `CPoseProducer::MakeSkinningPalette`.

The per-draw render block instance info: the instance's constant buffers, LOD state, and world
 transforms.

associated_functions.name: GetMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Writes the instance world transform for the given transform slot into `out` (also returned).
 The render blocks pass [`RenderContext::m_TransformIndex`](graphics_engine::graphics_engine::RenderContext::m_TransformIndex)
 as the slot for the current dispatch.

kind.doc:
The skinned character render block (the `Character` RBMDL block type). A character model is
 composed of one block per material; the same block objects are drawn for every pass, branching
 internally on [`RenderContext::m_RenderStatus`](graphics_engine::graphics_engine::RenderContext::m_RenderStatus)
 to select the shadow/depth-only path versus the full material path.

fields.name: m_SkinBatchesBegin
fields.doc: The `std::vector<CSkinBatch>` begin pointer.

fields.name: m_SkinBatchesEnd
fields.doc: The `std::vector<CSkinBatch>` end pointer.

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc:
Draws the block for the current pass. Shadow passes
 ([`RenderContext::m_RenderStatus`](graphics_engine::graphics_engine::RenderContext::m_RenderStatus) `& 6`)
 take a depth-only path with the depth vertex shaders; other passes run the full material
 setup.

associated_functions.name: DrawZ
associated_functions.doc: Draws the block for the Z/velocity prepass.

associated_functions.name: SetMatrixPalette
associated_functions.doc:
Uploads one batch's bone matrices to the vertex-program palette constants: for each batch
 slot, copies `matrices[BatchToSkeletonLookup[slot]]` into the constant registers starting
 at `register`. Called from the block's internal `DrawBatches` before each batch's draw.

kind.doc:
The skinned character skin render block (the `CharacterSkin` RBMDL block type): the skin-shaded
 variant of [`RenderBlockCharacter`], with the same batch and pass structure.

fields.name: m_SkinBatchesBegin
fields.doc: The `std::vector<CSkinBatch>` begin pointer.

fields.name: m_SkinBatchesEnd
fields.doc: The `std::vector<CSkinBatch>` end pointer.

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc: Draws the block for the current pass; see [`RenderBlockCharacter::Draw`].

associated_functions.name: DrawZ
associated_functions.doc: Draws the block for the Z/velocity prepass.

associated_functions.name: SetMatrixPalette
associated_functions.doc: See [`RenderBlockCharacter::SetMatrixPalette`].

kind.doc:
A skinned draw batch within a character render block. The vertex data references palette slots;
 `BatchToSkeletonLookup` maps each slot to its skeleton bone index when the palette is built
 (`SetMatrixPalette`), so the batch's lookup table enumerates exactly the bones its geometry is
 weighted to.

fields.name: Size
fields.doc: The batch's index count (indices, not triangles; `DrawBatches` divides by 3).

fields.name: Offset
fields.doc: The batch's start offset in the block's index buffer.

fields.name: m_RenderPasses
fields.doc:
The per-pass render-block-item lists: one vector of [`RenderPass`] pointers per pass id.
 [`DrawRenderPassRange`](RenderEngine::DrawRenderPassRange) and the per-frame list rotation walk
 this.

fields.name: m_ConstantBufferRingIndex
fields.doc:
The per-Draw constant-buffer ring index (feeding `CalculateConstantBufferIndices`): each `Draw`
 selects a constant-buffer pool slot from this and advances it, wrapping at the limit in the `u32`
 immediately after. It advances independently of the engine frame counters.

associated_functions.name: PostDraw
associated_functions.doc: The late render-pass step: finalizes and copies render targets under the context mutex.

associated_functions.name: DrawRenderPassRange
associated_functions.doc:
Draws every render block in the half-open pass-index range `[first, last)`: for each pass it
 walks the [`RenderPass`] list and vtable-dispatches each block.

associated_functions.name: DrawGBuffer
associated_functions.doc:
The GBuffer fill: binds two global textures, then draws the GBuffer pass range (the depth and
 velocity prefix, static and dynamic models, and decals).

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc:
Lighting, reflections, opaque, environment, water, and transparency: draws the scene pass
 range, then clears the global texture samplers.

associated_functions.name: DrawPosteffects
associated_functions.doc:
The post-effects pass: draws the `RP_POSTEFFECTS` range, whose block is
 [`RenderBlockPostEffects::Draw`].

associated_functions.name: SetGlobalShaderConstants
associated_functions.doc:
Uploads the global per-view constant buffer for the frame: lighting, fog, wetness, and the
 render camera's full (translation-bearing) view-projection and world position. This drives
 screen-space and non-geometry work, not opaque-geometry vertex placement.

associated_functions.name: ApplyJitterTransform
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame TAA jitter: forwards to [`PostEffectsManager::ApplySubsampleJitter`], which
 post-multiplies a sub-pixel clip-space translation onto `proj` only at the T2X resolve mode.

associated_functions.name: EraseAllDeletedRenderBlocks
associated_functions.doc:
Drains a separate deferred deletion list of render blocks, under its own critical section. Does
 not touch the per-pass draw lists.

kind.doc:
The flat, contiguous render-pass id enum. Every pass / first / last index in the render engine is
 one of these. The render engine draws by pass-index range: the GBuffer from `RP_Z_OCCLUDERS` to
 `RP_LAST_GBUFFER`, the scene from `RP_REFLECTIVE_WATER_PLANES` to `RP_LAST_MAIN`, and the
 post-effects at `RP_POSTEFFECTS`. Named [`RenderPassId`] to avoid clashing with the [`RenderPass`]
 type.

 Verified against the retail pass-name switch ([`GetRenderPassName`]): relative to the 2016 dump,
 retail inserts `RP_VEGETATION_TRANSPARENT_AOIT` at `0x74` (shifting everything above by one) and
 removes the dump-era `RP_PARTICLE_RIBBON`, leaving `0x82` unnamed.
The per-frame constant-buffer ring.

associated_functions.name: HandBackBuffers
associated_functions.doc: Recycles last frame's constant buffers from the in-use stack back to the free pool.

kind.doc:
The per-item info accompanying a render block on a draw list (transform, sort key, and the like).
 Opaque; handled only behind pointers.

One of a render pass's double-buffered render-block-item lists.

 [`Add`](RBILists::Add) appends entries; on overflow (`m_NumElements >= m_ListSize`) it spills into
 the global overflow list. [`DoDraw`](RenderPass::DoDraw) reads `min(m_ListSize, m_NumElements)`
 entries and never writes `m_NumElements`, so a populated list can be redrawn any number of times.

fields.name: m_List
fields.doc: The array of entries.

fields.name: m_ListSize
fields.doc: The capacity.

fields.name: m_NumElements
fields.doc: The live element count (volatile).

associated_functions.name: Add
associated_functions.doc: Appends one render-block-item, spilling to the global overflow list on capacity overflow.

kind.doc: One render block enqueued onto a draw list. Opaque; handled only behind pointers.

fields.name: m_CurrentAddList
fields.doc:
The list that new render-block-items append to this frame. Each rotation,
 [`SaveRenderFrameData`](RenderPass::SaveRenderFrameData) re-points it at the new parity's list
 and zeroes its count; zeroing that count is how a pass's draw-time additions are reset.

fields.name: m_StateFlags

associated_functions.name: SetupRenderFrameData
associated_functions.doc:
Appends `count` render-block-items (`items`) to the active draw / add lists (`a3` holds the
 lists). Static. Called per batch, including from CPU fragment worker threads, not once per
 frame.

associated_functions.name: SetRenderContextCamera
associated_functions.doc:
Fills the per-view render context from `camera` (the render camera, or a shadow or reflective
 light camera selected by the pass flags): the view, projection, and translation-free offset
 view-projection via [`CalculateOffsetViewProjectionMatrix`], the separate camera world
 position, and the parity-buffered shadow matrices. Static.

associated_functions.name: SaveRenderFrameData
associated_functions.doc:
The per-pass half of the list rotation, driven by the per-frame `CKeep1000Frames` call in the
 `CGraphicsEngine::Draw` prologue: points `m_CurrentAddList` at the new parity's list, the draw
 list at the other, and zeroes the new add-list's element count. Also snapshots the pass camera.

associated_functions.name: DoDraw
associated_functions.doc:
Draws the current draw list, walking `min(m_ListSize, m_NumElements)` blocks and
 vtable-dispatching each. Non-destructive -- never writes `m_NumElements`.

kind.doc: Render-pass state flags. Only the draw gate is identified; the remaining bits are unmapped.

One cascade's bookkeeping in [`ShadowManager`]: the render passes that draw its atlas slice,
 followed by the per-parity fit state.

fields.name: m_Passes
fields.doc:
The pair of render passes that draw this cascade's atlas slice (dynamic and static
 geometry). [`ShadowManager::CommitRenderPassSettings`] gates them per dispatch via
 [`RenderPassState::m_Enabled`](graphics_engine::render_pass::RenderPassState::m_Enabled).

kind.doc:
The cascaded sun-shadow system. Each sim frame, [`UpdateRender`](ShadowManager::UpdateRender)
 fits the scheduled cascades to the active camera and writes the parity-buffered fit data; each
 dispatch, [`CommitRenderPassSettings`](ShadowManager::CommitRenderPassSettings) enables the
 scheduled atlas passes. Cascade re-renders are amortised across frames by an update pattern, so
 an unscheduled cascade keeps its previous fit and contents.

fields.name: m_Enabled
fields.doc:
The settings-side enable flag. The per-frame [`UpdateRender`](ShadowManager::UpdateRender)
 syncs the engine to it via [`SetEnabled`](ShadowManager::SetEnabled) whenever it differs
 from the live state -- the graphics-menu path for toggling shadows.

fields.name: m_Cascades
fields.doc: The per-cascade slots (passes plus fit bookkeeping).

associated_functions.name: GetShadowFade
associated_functions.doc: The global sun-shadow fade factor staged into the shader constants.

associated_functions.name: CommitRenderPassSettings
associated_functions.doc:
The per-dispatch pass gate: clears every shadow pass's
 [`m_Enabled`](graphics_engine::render_pass::RenderPassState::m_Enabled) flag, then
 re-enables the passes the update pattern scheduled this frame and re-points their render
 targets by the frame parity.

associated_functions.name: SetEnabled
associated_functions.doc:
Creates or destroys the shadow render targets and passes; the settings path behind
 [`m_Enabled`](ShadowManager::m_Enabled).

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
The sim-side per-frame update: syncs [`m_Enabled`](ShadowManager::m_Enabled) via
 [`SetEnabled`](ShadowManager::SetEnabled), fits the scheduled cascades to
 [`CameraManager::m_ActiveCamera`](camera::camera_manager::CameraManager::m_ActiveCamera)
 (the fit frustum comes from that camera's `m_ProjectionF`), copies the unscheduled
 cascades' previous fits forward, writes the parity-indexed cascade transforms and per-slice
 matrices the draw side reads, and regenerates the cull planes.

kind.doc:
The screen-space ambient-occlusion render pass.

 It maintains a two-slot temporal AO history, blended in when `m_EnableTemporalFilter` is set. The
 history index advances once per render-block draw (an inlined `SetNextHistoryBuffer` at the end of
 the apply), not in the per-frame list rotation.

 The render block's final composite draw lives inside the `m_EnableTemporalFilter` branch, so
 clearing that flag disables the whole apply (no AO at all). `m_FirstPass` is the engine's "no valid
 history, compute fresh" lever: it skips the history resolve and weights the temporal shader fully
 toward the current AO. The render block clears it after the first apply.

fields.name: m_PrevFrameIndex
fields.doc:
The previously-resolved slot of the two-slot temporal AO history; the resolve samples it. It
 advances once per draw (the inlined `SetNextHistoryBuffer`), independent of `m_FirstPass`.

fields.name: m_CurrFrameIndex
fields.doc:
The current slot the resolve writes and the final composite samples; advances with
 [`m_PrevFrameIndex`](SSAOPass::m_PrevFrameIndex).

fields.name: m_EnableTemporalFilter
fields.doc:
Enables the temporal filter, blending the current AO against the previous frame's result
 through the history ping-pong. Defaults on. Also gates the render block's final composite draw,
 so clearing it disables AO entirely -- prefer `m_FirstPass` to reset the history.

fields.name: m_FirstPass
fields.doc:
The "no valid history" lever. When set, it skips the history resolve and weights the temporal
 shader fully toward the current AO; the render block clears it after the first apply.

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc: Sets up the render context and enqueues the AO render block onto the active draw list.

fields.name: m_Format
fields.doc: The `DXGI_FORMAT` value.

kind.doc:
A per-frame brightness histogram used for eye adaptation. Its occlusion-query bucket counts are
 what the exposure read-back consumes.

fields.name: m_NumPixelsInBuckets
fields.doc:
The per-bucket pixel counts: one occlusion query per bucket, counting samples whose luminance
 is at or above the bucket's threshold. Only the first `m_NumBuckets` entries are live.

fields.name: m_HistogramBrightPoint
fields.doc: The bright-point computed from the buckets, driving the exposure target.

fields.name: m_HistogramMidPoint
fields.doc: The mid-point computed from the buckets.

kind.doc:
An N-frame exposure smoother: a ring-buffer average that advances once per render, with no `dt`
 term.
HDR tone-mapping and auto-exposure (eye adaptation).

fields.name: m_Histogram
fields.doc:
The exposure-weighted histogram, filled by
 [`CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram`] and read by [`Update`](ToneMappingEffect::Update).

fields.name: m_HistogramPingPong
fields.doc:
The ping-pong selector for the exposure-weighted histogram metering, flipped each frame by
 [`Update`](ToneMappingEffect::Update).

fields.name: m_Histogram2
fields.doc:
The second histogram, metering raw scene brightness. Its `m_HistogramMidPoint` is the divisor
 of the auto-exposure target (`target = key / midpoint`), so this -- not `m_Histogram` -- is
 what the converged `m_CurrentExposure` actually tracks.

fields.name: m_NumBuckets
fields.doc: The active histogram bucket count.

fields.name: m_CurrentExposure
fields.doc:
The current clamped, smoothed auto-exposure multiplier. Written once per frame by
 [`Update`](ToneMappingEffect::Update) and read back into the next frame's
 [`GenerateHistogramForFinalScene`](ToneMappingEffect::GenerateHistogramForFinalScene) metering,
 closing the feedback loop.

associated_functions.name: GenerateHistogramForFinalScene
associated_functions.doc:
Builds the auto-exposure histogram for the final scene, writes the current histogram slot
 indices through `a6` and `a7`, and returns `a7`. Meters luminance through a shader fed the
 previous frame's `m_CurrentExposure`, so metering is exposure-weighted -- the histogram feeds
 back into the exposure that weights the next frame's metering.

associated_functions.name: DrawHistogramWindow
associated_functions.doc:
Meters the second, non-exposure-weighted scene-luminance histogram (`m_Histogram2`) at a fixed
 exposure of `1.0`, so it measures raw scene brightness -- the value
 [`Update`](ToneMappingEffect::Update) divides the auto-exposure target by. Runs once per
 dispatch in the post chain, like
 [`GenerateHistogramForFinalScene`](ToneMappingEffect::GenerateHistogramForFinalScene).

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame eye-adaptation step: runs [`CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram`] over both
 histograms, then writes the new `m_CurrentExposure` (the target is `m_AutoExposureKey` over the
 `m_Histogram2` mid-point, clamped, then adapted). Runs once per real frame.

kind.doc:
An action ID, indexing [`action_name_table`]. The numbering is fixed at build time, so action IDs
 can be hardcoded. The engine takes a raw int, and this enum is int-repr, so it converts cleanly
 into the action parameters.
The digital state of an [`InputDeviceEffector`].

variants.name: Idle
variants.doc: Idle, with no input this frame.

variants.name: Idle2
variants.doc: Idle, in the alternate idle slot.

variants.name: Clicked
variants.doc: Clicked this frame: a one-frame press edge.

variants.name: Frozen
variants.doc: Held latched by [`Freeze`](InputDeviceEffector::Freeze), ignoring the device poll.

kind.doc: Maps action IDs to effector slots.

One input effector slot. The pointer [`InputActionMap::GetActionEffector`] returns is the head of a
 linked-list node whose extra id / next fields follow this struct; reading the effector itself uses
 only these fields.

 **Provenance:** the layout is from the debug PDB, cross-checked against retail usage (`m_Value`,
 `m_State`, and `m_StateTime` offsets all match).

fields.name: m_Value
fields.doc:
The analog value, e.g. trigger pressure or stick axis. [`Click`](InputDeviceEffector::Click)
 sets it to `1.0`.

fields.name: m_ForceClick
fields.doc: Injects a click that survives the next device poll.

associated_functions.name: Click
associated_functions.doc:
Drives a one-frame press edge: sets `m_Value` to `1.0` and the state to
 [`EffectorState::Clicked`].

associated_functions.name: Press
associated_functions.doc: Sets `m_Value` and drives the [`EffectorState::Pressed`] state.

associated_functions.name: Freeze
associated_functions.doc: Latches into [`EffectorState::Frozen`], ignoring the device poll until cleared.

associated_functions.name: ForceClick
associated_functions.doc: Sets `m_ForceClick` so the click survives the next per-frame poll.

kind.doc:
The local player's action map: a write-side wrapper that drives effectors by [`Action`]. Invalid
 action IDs resolve to a shared null-effector sentinel, which the setters guard against.

associated_functions.name: GetActionEffector
associated_functions.doc:
The single-argument `CInputActionMap::GetActionEffector(int)` overload the wrapper's own
 setters use: resolves an action to its effector without a device index. The singleton
 pointer is the underlying `CInputActionMap` itself (established from `ForceSetPressed`,
 which passes it straight to the map's effector lookup), so the read-side lookup is exposed
 here. Returns the shared null-effector sentinel for invalid ids; callers null-check anyway.

associated_functions.name: ForceSetPressed
associated_functions.doc: Drives an analog or held action: sets the effector's value and presses it.

associated_functions.name: ForceSetClicked
associated_functions.doc: Drives a one-frame click for an action: a press edge that survives the poll.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame device poll: reads all devices, refreshes their effector states, and then runs
 [`UpdateForceClicks`](InputDeviceManager::UpdateForceClicks). Effector writes injected before
 this are overwritten by the poll, so inject after it instead.

associated_functions.name: UpdateForceClicks
associated_functions.doc: Applies the force-click flags latched on effectors so an injected click survives the poll.

kind.doc:
`SCharacterMovementSettings`: the per-character movement tuning block. Only passed through
 opaquely so far; its first float is the walk/run speed threshold the start-act selection
 compares against.

The locomotion move task active while the character is aim-relative (strafing). Mirrors
 [`NStateTask_InputLocoMoveTask`] but stays in the aim-relative acts as long as an aim bit is set.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame update. Like [`NStateTask_InputLocoMoveTask::Update`] it re-reads
 [`Character::m_AimFlags`] each frame and dispatches to the strafe or run acts, so dropping the
 aim bits here falls back to run/steer.

kind.doc:
The locomotion move task active when the character is on foot and *not* holding an aim. Its
 [`Update`](NStateTask_InputLocoMoveTask::Update) reads [`Character::m_AimFlags`] and dispatches
 to run/steer versus strafe locomotion.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame update. Reads the blackboard move direction and speed, then branches on
 [`Character::m_AimFlags`] ([`m_AimingWeapon`](character::character::AimState::m_AimingWeapon) /
 [`m_AimingGrapple`](character::character::AimState::m_AimingGrapple)): with neither set it queues
 [`QueueMoveActions`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::QueueMoveActions) (run — the body steers toward the
 movement vector), otherwise it queues the aim-relative (strafe) acts. This branch is the
 concrete point where on-foot movement becomes third-person steer instead of FPS-style strafe.

kind.doc:
The locomotion task that reads the movement effectors and writes the move direction to the
 character blackboard. Like the other `NStateTask` functions, `self` is the *character* -- the
 task types are namespaces over free functions that take the character as `this` (the blackboard
 at `+0x2060` and the body forward at `+0x2850` are read straight off it).

associated_functions.name: SetupTargetDir
associated_functions.doc:
Reads the move effectors via the action-map effector lookup, rotates the raw vector
 camera-relative (via the camera input matrix), and writes the previous and new move
 directions to the character blackboard. `target_dir` is in/out: the previous direction on
 entry, the new one on return. Below the input deadzone the *previous* direction (or the
 body forward) is re-written -- the blackboard move direction is always a unit vector and
 carries no input-held information.

kind.doc:
Free helpers that queue the concrete locomotion animation acts. The queued act encodes the
 movement style: [`QueueMoveActions`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::QueueMoveActions) steers the body
 toward the movement direction, while the aim-relative variants keep the body facing the aim and
 play directional strafe animations.

associated_functions.name: QueueMoveActions
associated_functions.doc:
Queues the run/steer acts (`ACT_MOVE_NO_AIM` and friends). The body turns to face the
 movement direction, so directional input rotates the whole character — the third-person
 on-foot behavior that reads as a "tank turn" in VR.

associated_functions.name: QueueAimRelativeMoveActions
associated_functions.doc:
Queues the aim-relative (strafe) acts. It derives the facing reference from the weapon-aim
 target when [`m_AimingWeapon`](character::character::AimState::m_AimingWeapon) is set (or the
 grapple target for [`m_AimingGrapple`](character::character::AimState::m_AimingGrapple)),
 and falls back to the character's own current forward when neither is set — so this alone
 does not turn the body toward the camera without an aim reference.

associated_functions.name: QueueTransitionToAimRelativeMoveActions
associated_functions.doc:
Queues the one-shot transition into the aim-relative (strafe) acts, played on the frame the
 character starts aiming.

associated_functions.name: EvaluateCharacterOrientation
associated_functions.doc:
Computes the character's new orientation for this frame; the body-yaw executor, called from
 [`NStateTask_MovementLocomotionTask::Update`]. With `track_face_dir` clear (and outside a
 gating animation segment) the rotation comes from the animation root motion
 (`CAnimationControl::GetDeltaRotation`) — the run-mode steer. With it set, the target face
 direction is read from the character blackboard (id `736589998`) and the body is yawed
 toward it: snapped directly when `snap_to_face_dir` is set (gated on the blend being
 finished when `wait_for_blend` is also set), otherwise rate-limited to `max_step_deg`
 degrees per call — the aim-mode camera tracking. `max_step_deg` must be positive on the
 tracking path (it divides by it). `dt` is only used for the attached-to-vehicle up-vector
 lerp.

associated_functions.name: EvaluateCharacterOrientationMS
associated_functions.doc:
The model-space variant of
 [`EvaluateCharacterOrientation`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::EvaluateCharacterOrientation), also
 called from the movement task and from the grapple reel-in controller. The bools are
 believed to match the world-space variant minus the blend gate; unverified.

associated_functions.name: EvaluateCharacterDisplacement
associated_functions.doc:
Computes this frame's movement direction from the animation root motion and the new body
 orientation. Called from [`NStateTask_MovementLocomotionTask::Update`], which normalizes
 `out_direction`, scales it by
 [`EvaluateCharacterSpeed`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::EvaluateCharacterSpeed), and writes the
 result as the character's physics velocity -- so overriding `out_direction` after the call
 redirects the movement without touching the speed envelope. The bools and `out_secondary`
 are unmapped.

associated_functions.name: EvaluateCharacterSpeed
associated_functions.doc:
Computes this frame's movement speed for the character: the magnitude of the animation
 control's raw root velocity -- so the speed envelope, including the ramp-up through the
 start acts, comes from the animation clips themselves. With `use_blackboard_override` set,
 the blackboard float id `1707123197` replaces the result when present. Multiplied onto the
 normalized displacement direction by the movement task.

associated_functions.name: QueueStarts
associated_functions.doc:
Queues the directional run-start (wind-up) acts: measures the XZ angle from the body
 forward to the blackboard move direction, writes the residual angle correction, and queues
 the matching start act (forward/left/right/180, with stunt variants), choosing the
 walk-versus-run flavor by comparing `speed` against the settings' threshold. The start
 clips' root velocity ramping from zero is the on-foot acceleration.

associated_functions.name: QueueStops
associated_functions.doc:
Queues the stop acts for run-mode locomotion; taken by the input tasks when the blackboard
 speed is not positive or the stop validation triggers.

associated_functions.name: QueueStopTurns
associated_functions.doc:
The aim-mode variant of [`QueueStops`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::QueueStops): stop-and-turn acts
 that settle the body toward the aim.

associated_functions.name: GetAimMoveAngle
associated_functions.doc:
The XZ angle (degrees) from `move_dir` to the aim reference direction: the vector from the
 character to the player's aim-target position (or the camera forward while planting an
 explosive), both rotated into the character's local frame when the relevant state flag is
 set. Consumed by the aim-relative act dispatchers
 ([`QueueAimRelativeMoveActions`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::QueueAimRelativeMoveActions),
 [`QueueStopTurns`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::QueueStopTurns), and the on-spot turn queuers) to
 select directional acts and on-spot turns. `move_dir` is a by-value `CVector3f` in the
 source, passed by pointer under the x64 ABI.

kind.doc:
The movement actuator task: applies this frame's locomotion to the character, computing the
 body orientation via
 [`EvaluateCharacterOrientation`](NStateTask_LocoUtil::EvaluateCharacterOrientation) (or the MS
 variant) and the translation from the animation root motion.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc: The per-frame update.

kind.doc:
The opaque per-task state-machine context passed to character and task update functions. Used only
 behind pointers; the layout is not yet mapped.

associated_functions.name: as_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Raw pointer to the row-major data, for passing to engine APIs.

associated_functions.name: as_mut_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Mutable raw pointer to the row-major data.

kind.doc:
The engine's 4x4 matrix, in the D3D convention: row-major storage with row vectors (a point is a
 row vector left-multiplied by the matrix, `clip = p * M`).

 The 16 floats are four rows; a world or camera transform's rows are its basis vectors:
 `data[0..2]` is right (+X), `data[4..6]` is up (+Y), `data[8..10]` is the +Z basis (so forward is
 `-data[8..10]`), and `data[12..14]` is the translation. Right-handed, Y-up.

 The glam conversions below bridge to glam's column-vector convention by transposing (row-major rows
 become glam columns), so glam matrix math on a converted [`Matrix4`] works without an explicit
 transpose. To build an engine transform in glam, set the basis vectors as the glam *columns*
 (right, up, -forward, translation) and use `to_cols_array`.

associated_functions.name: as_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Raw pointer to the row-major data, for passing to engine APIs.

associated_functions.name: as_mut_ptr
associated_functions.doc: Mutable raw pointer to the row-major data.

associated_functions.name: Multiply4x4
associated_functions.doc:
The engine's 4x4 product, `result = a * b` (row-major: result row `i` is `a`'s row `i` times
 `b`). For example, `Multiply4x4(View, Proj, ViewProjection)` gives `VP = View * Proj` and
 `clip = p * View * Proj`. Static.

kind.doc:
A `std::string` in the MSVC layout: the small-string-optimization buffer/pointer union
 (`_Bx`), then the length (`_Mysize`) and capacity (`_Myres`). The string is stored inline in
 [`buffer`](String::buffer) while `capacity < 16`; otherwise the first eight bytes of the
 buffer are a heap pointer to the character data.

fields.name: buffer
fields.doc: `_Bx`: the inline character buffer, or (heap case) a `char*` in the first eight bytes.

fields.name: size
fields.doc: `_Mysize`: the length in bytes, excluding the NUL terminator.

fields.name: capacity
fields.doc:
`_Myres`: the allocated capacity in bytes, excluding the NUL terminator. `>= 16` means the
 data lives on the heap.

kind.doc:
A `std::vector<T>` in the MSVC layout: the `_Myfirst` / `_Mylast` / `_Myend` pointers. This build's
 vectors carry a trailing allocator pointer beyond the three, so the type is sized to match (e.g.
 when used as a fixed-array element).

fields.name: begin
fields.doc: `_Myfirst`: the start of the element array.

fields.name: end
fields.doc: `_Mylast`: one past the last live element.

fields.name: capacity_end
fields.doc: `_Myend`: one past the allocated capacity.

associated_functions.name: len
associated_functions.doc: Number of live elements.

associated_functions.name: is_empty
associated_functions.doc: `true` if there are no live elements.

kind.doc:
A node in the display tree returned by `Movie::GetDisplayObjectsTree`. Inherits from
 [`RefCountImpl`] and embeds a `Scaleform::String` for the name and a
 `Scaleform::ArrayDataBase<Ptr<AmpMovieObjectDesc>>` for children.

 Layout verified from `GetChildDescTree` at `0x141_A30_410`.

fields.name: name
fields.doc:
The clip's instance name, or `"Unnamed"`. A `Scaleform::String` (heap-allocated with SSO);
 the first 8 bytes are a `*const c_char` pointing into the internal buffer.

fields.name: children
fields.doc:
Pointer to the child array. Each element is a `Ptr<AmpMovieObjectDesc>` (a raw pointer
 to another `AmpMovieObjectDesc`).

fields.name: child_count
fields.doc: Number of children in [`children`](AmpMovieObjectDesc::children).

fields.name: child_capacity
fields.doc: Allocated capacity of [`children`](AmpMovieObjectDesc::children).

associated_functions.name: AddRef
associated_functions.doc: Increments the reference count atomically.

associated_functions.name: Release
associated_functions.doc:
Decrements the reference count atomically. When it reaches zero, calls the virtual
 destructor (vtable slot 0).

functions.name: destructor
functions.doc: Virtual destructor, slot 0. Called by `Release` when the refcount reaches zero.

kind.doc:
Scaleform `GFx::Movie` -- the public interface backed by `MovieRoot` (AS3). The vtable is
 `MovieRoot`'s at `0x142_621_780`.

associated_functions.name: SetVariable
associated_functions.doc:
Sets an AS3 variable by clip path. The path is a dot-separated string like
 `"root.MCI_hud.MCI_poi_stage._visible"`. The value is a `GFx::Value` (a tagged union for
 bool/int/float/string/object). `SetVarType` controls search scope (0 = full path).

associated_functions.name: GetVariable
associated_functions.doc:
Reads an AS3 variable by clip path into a `GFx::Value`. Returns false if the path is not
 found.

associated_functions.name: Invoke
associated_functions.doc:
Invokes an AS3 method on the movie's root timeline by name. This is the call path used by
 the engine's `CUIBase::Invoke`. The `args` are a `GFx::Value` array; `result` receives the
 return value (or null). Note the parameter order: `result` comes *before* `args`, unlike
 the engine's `CUIBase::Invoke` wrapper (which also adds a timeout).

functions.name: GetDisplayObjectsTree
functions.doc:
Recursively walks the runtime display tree from the root movie clip: returns a tree of
 [`AmpMovieObjectDesc`] nodes, each carrying the clip's instance name (or `"Unnamed"`)
 and a child array, calling `DisplayObject::GetName` on every child and recursing into
 containers. `heap` is the Scaleform `MemoryHeap*` used for the allocations. Slot 9
 (offset `0x48`); the implementation is at `0x141_BED_530`.

fields.name: GetDisplayObjectsTree
fields.doc:
Recursively walks the runtime display tree from the root movie clip: returns a tree of
 [`AmpMovieObjectDesc`] nodes, each carrying the clip's instance name (or `"Unnamed"`)
 and a child array, calling `DisplayObject::GetName` on every child and recursing into
 containers. `heap` is the Scaleform `MemoryHeap*` used for the allocations. Slot 9
 (offset `0x48`); the implementation is at `0x141_BED_530`.

fields.name: ref_count
fields.doc: Atomic reference count. Incremented by `AddRef`, decremented by `Release`.

associated_functions.name: AddRef
associated_functions.doc: Increments the reference count atomically.

associated_functions.name: Release
associated_functions.doc:
Decrements the reference count atomically. When it reaches zero, calls the virtual
 destructor (vtable slot 0).

functions.name: destructor
functions.doc: Virtual destructor, slot 0. Called by `Release` when the refcount reaches zero.

fields.name: destructor
fields.doc: Virtual destructor, slot 0. Called by `Release` when the refcount reaches zero.

kind.doc:
Static clip names placed on the root timeline of `ui/hud.gfx` via PlaceObject2 tags. The root
 movie (`ui/root.gfx`) creates its clips entirely through ActionScript, so it has no static
 PlaceObject2 tags; `hud.gfx` uses a mix of static placement and dynamic creation.

 | Instance name | Depth | Char ID |
 |---|---|---|
 | `MCI_hud` | 1432 | 1548 |
 | `MCI_poi_stage` | 180 | 1066 |
 | `MCI_weapon_selection_wheel` | 182 | 1078 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_center` | 1 | 1065 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_top_left` | 839 | 1538 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_top_middle` | 636 | 1532 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_top_right` | 562 | 1395 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_bottom_left` | 450 | 1330 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_bottom_middle` | 307 | 1286 |
 | `MCI_safe_area_bottom_right` | 246 | 1161 |

 UI movie registry from `root.gfx` maps each `CUIBase` subclass to its `.gfx` file:
 `CSharedLibUI` -> `shared_lib`, `COverlayUI` -> `overlay`, `CHUDUI` -> `hud`, `CIntroUI` ->
 `intro`, `CTitleUI` -> `title`, `CPauseUI` -> `pause`, `CTutorialsUI` -> `tutorials`,
 `CCreditsUI` -> `credits`, `CLobbyUI` -> `lobby`, `CMainUI` -> `main`, `CCommLinkUI` ->
 `comm_link`, `CCommCollectiblesUI` -> `comm_collectibles`, `CCommCommunityUI` ->
 `comm_community`, `CCommMapUI` -> `comm_map`, `CCommSkillUI` -> `comm_skill`,
 `CCommStatsUI` -> `comm_stats`, `CCommBragsFeatsUI` -> `comm_brags_feats`,
 `CCommStoreUI` -> `comm_store`, `CRewardUI` -> `reward`, `CROMUI` -> (none).

A Scaleform render buffer: what [`UIManager::m_RenderBuffer`] points at. It holds the
 render-target and depth-stencil views the UI HAL renders into; [`UpdateData`](RenderTargetData::UpdateData)
 rebinds those views, and is not tied to startup.

fields.name: m_BufferWidth
fields.doc:
The render-target buffer width. [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT) builds the
 buffer square, setting width = height = its side argument.

fields.name: m_BufferHeight
fields.doc:
The render-target buffer height; [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT) sets it equal to
 the width (a square buffer).

fields.name: m_ViewRectRight
fields.doc: The view rectangle's right edge (x2 = width).

fields.name: m_ViewRectBottom
fields.doc:
The view rectangle's bottom edge (y2); [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT) sets it
 equal to the width.

associated_functions.name: UpdateData
associated_functions.doc:
Rebinds the buffer's views, releasing the old ones and adding a reference to the new. `self`
 is the render buffer itself ([`UIManager::m_RenderBuffer`]); the call reaches its inner
 view-holder internally. Passing `depth` as null leaves the depth buffer unchanged.

kind.doc:
The screen-position code returned by the UI world-to-screen and marker placement: on-screen, an
 off-screen clamped edge or corner, off-screen in front (no clamp), or off-screen behind.

The Scaleform-backed UI manager, the single concrete instance behind `IUIManager`. It renders the
 HUD into the engine surface. [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT) builds its render
 buffer, [`ComputeMovieSizeOnViewSize`](UIManager::ComputeMovieSizeOnViewSize) sizes the movie
 render rectangle, [`SetMovieViewport`](UIManager::SetMovieViewport) sets the Scaleform movie
 viewport, and [`ComputeSafeArea`](UIManager::ComputeSafeArea) derives the UI safe area.

fields.name: m_MovieScaleWidth
fields.doc:
The movie render rectangle's width, from the engine's `MovieScaleInfo`. The movie is rendered
 into a `m_MovieScaleWidth` x `m_MovieScaleHeight` rectangle, centered within the viewport
 passed to [`SetMovieViewport`](UIManager::SetMovieViewport).
 [`ComputeMovieSizeOnViewSize`](UIManager::ComputeMovieSizeOnViewSize) recomputes it from the
 device resolution and the movie stage size.

fields.name: m_MovieScaleHeight
fields.doc: The movie render rectangle's height; see [`m_MovieScaleWidth`](UIManager::m_MovieScaleWidth).

fields.name: m_Loader
fields.doc:
The Scaleform `GFx::Loader`, created in `InitializeSystem`. Loads `.gfx` files via
 `Loader::CreateMovie`.

fields.name: m_MovieDef
fields.doc: The `GFx::MovieDef` for `ui/root.gfx` -- the definition object, not the live instance.

fields.name: m_Movie
fields.doc:
The live `GFx::Movie` instance (a `MovieRoot` under the hood), created by
 `MovieDef::CreateInstance` in `InitializeSystem`. All `CUIBase` subclasses share this
 single movie.

fields.name: m_RenderBuffer
fields.doc:
The Scaleform render buffer the UI HAL renders into, set up by
 [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT). [`RenderTargetData::UpdateData`] rebinds which
 views it renders into.

fields.name: m_CachedStageWidth
fields.doc:
The movie stage's authored width (`m_CachedStageSize.x`), refreshed every frame from the loaded
 movie. The world-to-screen mapping ([`Convert3DCoords`](UIManager::Convert3DCoords)) maps NDC
 into this.

fields.name: m_CachedStageHeight
fields.doc: The movie stage's authored height; see [`m_CachedStageWidth`](UIManager::m_CachedStageWidth).

fields.name: m_CachedViewportWidth
fields.doc:
The cached viewport width (`m_CachedViewportSize.x`), refreshed every frame from the graphics
 device's display resolution. [`ComputeSafeArea`](UIManager::ComputeSafeArea) reads it to expand
 the UI safe area to the viewport's aspect.

fields.name: m_CachedViewportHeight
fields.doc: The cached viewport height; see [`m_CachedViewportWidth`](UIManager::m_CachedViewportWidth).

fields.name: m_CachedViewportRatio
fields.doc:
The cached viewport aspect ratio (`m_CachedViewportSize.y / .x`, i.e. device height / width),
 refreshed every frame from the graphics device. [`Convert3DCoords`](UIManager::Convert3DCoords)
 uses it -- not the width/height fields -- to aspect-correct world-to-screen.

associated_functions.name: InitPlatformRT
associated_functions.doc:
Binds the UI render target: builds a [`RenderTargetData`] from the engine surface's
 render-target and depth-stencil views via [`RenderTargetData::UpdateData`]. Called at startup
 and on every device or resolution reset; `a2` carries the target side length (the buffer is
 square: width = height = a2). This only creates the render buffer -- the Scaleform movie's
 viewport is set separately by [`SetMovieViewport`](UIManager::SetMovieViewport).

associated_functions.name: RestoreAfterReset
associated_functions.doc:
Re-runs [`InitPlatformRT`](UIManager::InitPlatformRT) after a device or resolution reset. Also
 recomputes the movie size, sets the movie viewport, and recomputes the safe area. Gated by an
 internal reset counter, so a direct call may no-op if the counter is not at 1.

associated_functions.name: ComputeMovieSizeOnViewSize
associated_functions.doc:
Recomputes the movie render rectangle ([`m_MovieScaleWidth`](UIManager::m_MovieScaleWidth) /
 [`m_MovieScaleHeight`](UIManager::m_MovieScaleHeight)). It first refreshes the cached stage and
 viewport sizes from the live device resolution (via an internal `UpdateCachedValues`), then
 sizes the movie rectangle from them, so the rectangle always reflects the device aspect.

associated_functions.name: SetMovieViewport
associated_functions.doc:
Sets the Scaleform movie's viewport to `width` x `height`, centering the movie (of size
 [`m_MovieScaleWidth`](UIManager::m_MovieScaleWidth) x
 [`m_MovieScaleHeight`](UIManager::m_MovieScaleHeight)) within it at offset
 `((width - m_MovieScaleWidth) / 2, (height - m_MovieScaleHeight) / 2)`. This is the viewport the
 Scaleform HAL renders into.

associated_functions.name: ComputeSafeArea
associated_functions.doc: Computes the safe-area rectangle from the current movie viewport.

associated_functions.name: ComputeGameViewArea
associated_functions.doc: Computes the game view area from the current movie viewport.

associated_functions.name: Convert3DCoords
associated_functions.doc:
World-to-screen: projects `world` through `vp`, divides by w, aspect-corrects, and maps NDC to
 pixels. Returns false when the point is behind the camera.

associated_functions.name: Get2DInfo
associated_functions.doc:
Marker placement: [`Convert3DCoords`](UIManager::Convert3DCoords) with the supplied `vp`, plus
 an on-screen test and an off-screen edge-clamp via [`ClampToScreen`](UIManager::ClampToScreen).
 Gameplay markers route through here.

associated_functions.name: ClampToScreen
associated_functions.doc:
Clamps an off-screen point to the screen-rect edge or corner and sets the position code.
 Static.

associated_functions.name: StartRender
associated_functions.doc: Begins the UI render job, posting to the async render thread.

associated_functions.name: SyncRender
associated_functions.doc: A barrier that spin-waits until the async UI render job has finished.

associated_functions.name: Submit
associated_functions.doc: The final commit: submits the render HAL's output under a graphics scoped lock.

associated_functions.name: RenderStaticBackGround
associated_functions.doc: Draws the pause / menu static background.

associated_functions.name: RenderOffScreenTextures
associated_functions.doc: Renders Scaleform UI to offscreen textures for in-world screens. This is not the HUD.

associated_functions.name: IsUsingStaticBackGround
associated_functions.doc: Whether a static background is being shown.

functions.name: NAutoAimToTarget_Update
functions.doc: The character-state task that overrides the aim direction toward a locked target.

functions.name: NHandIKTask_Update
functions.doc: The per-frame hand-IK driver. Sources its targets from weapon grip positions.

functions.name: SetListenerTransform
functions.doc: Sets the 3D audio listener pose and velocity. Free function.

functions.name: CpuFragmentWaitUntilSignalIsNonZero
functions.doc:
Spin-wait (pumping other ready fragments) until `*signal` is non-zero -- one of Avalanche's CPU
 job-fragment primitives, the engine's drain for an outstanding async fragment. For example the
 draw-dispatch fragment signals completion at `GraphicsEngine::m_DrawThreadWorkSignal` (`+0x30`). It
 returns immediately when the signal is already non-zero, so it is only safe to call when the
 matching fragment was actually kicked: below two primary threads the work runs inline and the signal
 is never raised, so guard the call with [`CpuPrimaryCount`] to avoid spinning forever.

functions.name: CpuPrimaryCount
functions.doc:
The number of primary job-worker threads. The engine only dispatches the async draw fragment (and
 only waits on its signal) when this is greater than `1`; at or below it the draw runs inline and the
 completion signal is never raised, so any wait on that signal must be guarded by this count.

functions.name: send_event_msg_impl
functions.doc:
`NEvent::CSendEvent::SendMsg(const char*, void*)`: fires a named engine event to every
 subscribed receiver, with an optional message payload (null for fire-and-forget). This is the
 engine's own idiom for global events — `CDialogue::Play` fires `on_play_dialogue` through it —
 and the sanctioned way to drive systems that subscribe named events in their `Init`, such as
 the weather events `CWeatherController` subscribes (`weather_sunny`, `weather_rain`,
 `weather_snow`, `weather_restore`, `weather_instant`, `cloud_base`, and `cloud_height`).

functions.name: GetMasterContext
functions.doc: Returns the master context, the wrapper around the D3D11 immediate context. `device` is unused.

functions.name: DrawIndexed
functions.doc: An indexed draw.

functions.name: Draw
functions.doc: A non-indexed draw.

functions.name: DrawInstanced
functions.doc: An instanced draw.

functions.doc: An indexed instanced draw.

functions.doc: A GPU-driven instanced draw.

functions.name: DrawIndexedInstancedIndirect
functions.doc: A GPU-driven indexed instanced draw.

functions.name: Dispatch
functions.doc: A compute dispatch.

functions.name: DispatchIndirect
functions.doc: A GPU-driven compute dispatch.

functions.name: SetRenderSetup
functions.doc: Binds a render setup, the render-target configuration a pass draws into.

functions.name: Clear
functions.doc: Clears the currently-bound render setup. `color` is a 4-float RGBA pointer and may be null.

functions.name: CopySurfaceToTexture
functions.doc: Copies one surface into another texture.

functions.name: ResolveSurface
functions.doc: Resolves an MSAA surface.

functions.name: GetRTVFromSurface
functions.doc: Returns the surface's render-target view.

functions.name: GetDSVFromSurface
functions.doc: Returns the surface's depth-stencil view.

functions.name: CreateFragmentProgram
functions.doc:
The leaf fragment-program creator: it wraps `ID3D11Device::CreatePixelShader` over
 `params.m_Code`/`params.m_Size`. `CreatePixelShader` copies the bytecode, so a hook may substitute a
 patched copy that only has to outlive the call. Static (no `this`); the first argument is the
 graphics device.

functions.name: graphics_flip
functions.doc: The low-level present.

functions.name: GetRenderPassName
functions.doc:
The debug name for a render-pass id, from the engine's pass-name switch (the ground truth the
 [`RenderPassId`] values are verified against). Returns `"NONE"` for unnamed indices.

functions.name: CalculateOffsetViewProjectionMatrix
functions.doc:
Copies `src` (a view matrix), zeroes its translation row, and multiplies by `proj`, writing the
 translation-free offset view-projection into `dst`. This is the view-projection that camera-relative
 opaque geometry actually uses; the camera world position is supplied separately. Static.

functions.name: CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram
functions.doc: Computes the histogram mid and bright points, the per-frame eye-adaptation lerp.
